title: Bridge Essay: Gut microbiome is associated with insula structure in neonates.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Body-energy** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Interoception — visceral perception, cardiac interoception, insula, vagal signaling, felt aliveness.

**Gut microbiome is associated with insula structure in neonates.**. Physiol Behav.. PMID: 40532867. DOI: 10.1016/j.physbeh.2025.115001. The infant brain and gut microbiome both develop rapidly in early infancy. Growing evidence indicates that the gut microbiome plays acritical role in shaping neurodevelopment early in life, possibly through effects on brain regions involved in affective, interoceptive, and sensory processing. This study used magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) and whole genome sequencing of fecal samples to examine associations between the gut microbiome and brain structures in infants at 2 weeks of age.
